definition: Assembling specific XML document structural features (CDATA section node, element with multiple namespace declarations, namespace-qualified self-closing element) requires multi-byte parser token comparisons that VP gradient climbs incrementally and I2S resolves with exact token substitution, with neither technique alone reliably constructing the required node-tree shape.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient preserves inputs progressively closer to matching the multi-byte '<![CDATA[' parser token comparisons, letting the corpus climb toward a valid CDATA construct that dispatches to the target switch case.
signature.one_line: VP gradient climbs multi-byte CDATA token comparisons to reach XML_CDATA_SECTION_NODE switch arm

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must parse into a DOM tree containing a CDATA section node, requiring a literal <![CDATA[ construct in well-formed XML

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Bytes that drive the libxml2 parser to allocate an XML_CDATA_SECTION_NODE, i.e. a recognizable multi-byte '<![CDATA[' literal embedded in a well-formed document so the saver's switch dispatches to case XML_CDATA_SECTION_NODE at line 988.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 8/10 and actually executes the CDATA arm (entry-line hits>=1; the W column shows L988 onward marked [W]); the CMP_MAP gradient feedback preserves intermediate inputs that get progressively closer to matching the parser's multi-byte token comparisons, building the '<![CDATA[' / '<?xml' / '<!DOCTYPE' skeleton seen in the winning seed.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolves only 1/10 and is blocked 9/10; lacking the CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback it gets no reward for partial matches of the multi-byte CDATA token, so it never reliably assembles the literal and the saver dispatches to other case arms (L988 stays False=194 on the L side).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The decisive technique is the value_profile CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient unique to value_profile_cmplog over cmplog; it rewards inputs whose CMP operands edge toward matching the parser's CDATA/element token comparisons, letting the corpus climb toward a valid '<![CDATA[' construct that cmplog (no CMP_MAP) cannot ratchet toward.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is the CDATA-section case arm of the XML serializer's node-type switch.
Reaching the arm requires the node's content, and the CDATA serialization scans for the ']]>' end token, implying a real CDATA construct was parsed.
On the winner the CDATA arm and its body are executed; on the loser the case label is taken False 194 times (never the CDATA node).
value_profile_cmplog wins over cmplog with the delta being the value_profile technique.
value_profile_cmplog wins over value_profile with the delta being the I2S technique.
cmplog resolves only 1/10 while value_profile_cmplog resolves 8/10 at this branch.
value_profile resolves only 2/10 while value_profile_cmplog resolves 8/10 at this branch.
The value_profile CMP_MAP rewards inputs whose operands get closer to matching, preserving partial-match progress toward a multi-byte token.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile has no I2SRandReplace mutator, so it cannot splice exact comparands.
I2SRandReplace scans the input for one operand and substitutes the other operand at those offsets, synthesizing the exact literal the parser tests for.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: The winning seed is well-formed XML carrying an xml declaration and DOCTYPE, the kind of structure the parser needs to build a typed node tree.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose CDATA arm is gated only on a single-byte comparison (not a multi-byte literal) showing no value_profile_cmplog advantage over either cmplog or value_profile would refute the claim that the divergence is driven by assembling a multi-byte CDATA token.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: I cannot directly confirm from the byte diff that the winning seeds literally contain '<![CDATA[' bytes; the requirement is inferred from the saver case arm plus the well-formed XML/DOCTYPE structure of the winning seed.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace splices the exact '<![CDATA[' literal (logged CDATA/element token comparands) into the input so the parser builds a CDATA section node, reaching the switch-case arm that value_profile stumbles onto only rarely.
signature.one_line: I2S plants '<![CDATA[' literal to build CDATA node and hit switch case

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must parse into a DOM tree containing a CDATA section node, requiring a literal <![CDATA[ construct in well-formed XML

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same requirement: bytes parsing into a tree with an XML_CDATA_SECTION_NODE, which depends on placing the exact multi-byte literal '<![CDATA[' (and surrounding well-formed XML) into the input.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 8/10; its I2SRandReplace mutator reads the CmpLogObserver buffer and splices the exact comparand the parser tests for (the CDATA/element token bytes) directly into the input at matching offsets, so the precise '<![CDATA[' literal appears verbatim. The winning seed's ancestor chain contains I2S-floor dash rows, confirming an I2S find.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: value_profile resolves only 2/10 and is blocked 8/10; it has no I2SRandReplace mutator (havoc+token only), so it must stumble onto the exact multi-byte CDATA literal by chance and rarely does, leaving the saver to dispatch to other case arms.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The decisive technique is cmplog's I2SRandReplace input-to-state substitution present in value_profile_cmplog but absent in value_profile; it copies the logged CDATA/element comparand into the input at matching offsets, directly synthesizing the literal token the parser needs to build a CDATA node.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is the CDATA-section case arm of the XML serializer's node-type switch.
Reaching the arm requires the node's content, and the CDATA serialization scans for the ']]>' end token, implying a real CDATA construct was parsed.
On the winner the CDATA arm and its body are executed; on the loser the case label is taken False 194 times (never the CDATA node).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose CDATA arm is gated only on a single-byte comparison (not a multi-byte literal) showing no value_profile_cmplog advantage over either cmplog or value_profile would refute the claim that the divergence is driven by assembling a multi-byte CDATA token.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads CmpLogObserver and splices XML keyword/token bytes into the input, repairing the prologue enough to drive a second xmlNewNs call, taking the false (non-NULL nsDef) path on the second invocation.
signature.one_line: I2S repairs XML prologue tokens to trigger second namespace definition call

analysis.summary_one_line: An XML element carrying two or more namespace declarations so xmlNewNs sees a non-NULL node->nsDef and appends a second namespace

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input must produce an element node that already has a first namespace (node->nsDef != NULL) and then declare a SECOND, distinctly-prefixed namespace on the same element, requiring well-formed multi-byte tokens like "<?xml version=\"1.0\"?>", "<!DOCTYPE", and a parseable xmlns attribute chain so SAX2 calls xmlNewNs twice on the same node.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile_cmplog winner seeds carry an intact, in-order XML prologue ("<?xml version=\"1.0\"?>", "<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM") at offsets 0x08+, which the I2SRandReplace mutator assembled by substituting CMP operands; the prologue parses far enough to drive a second xmlNewNs call and take the false (non-NULL nsDef) side, hitting it 6 times on average.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser seeds are shorter and structurally mangled (e.g. 97/156-byte seeds with byte runs like 0x13 repeats and corrupted prologue ordering), so the parser at most reaches the first namespace declaration; the loser only resolves the branch in 2/10 trials with avg hitcount 1.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and splices logged comparison operands (XML keyword/token bytes) into the input at matching offsets, repairing the multi-byte structural tokens that gate reaching a second xmlNewNs call; value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and cannot reconstruct those exact token sequences.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked side is the false branch of node->nsDef == NULL, taken only when the element already has a namespace, i.e. a second namespace is being added.
The false-side body that appends a second namespace (the winner-only region) is the else clause walking node->nsDef and linking cur via prev->next.
value_profile_cmplog beats value_profile on the I2S delta, resolving 9/10 vs 2/10 with higher branch hitcount.
value_profile_cmplog beats cmplog on the value_profile delta, resolving 9/10 vs 0/10.
The I2S winner pair shows winner hitcount 6 vs loser hitcount 1 on the branch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=6  loser=1

evidence_trail.claim: The value_profile winner pair shows winner hitcount 6 vs loser hitcount 0 on the branch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=6  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds carry an intact in-order XML prologue with version declaration and DOCTYPE tokens.
Winner seeds contain the DOCTYPE SYSTEM structural tokens needed to parse far enough to reach a second xmlNewNs call.
Loser seeds are short and structurally mangled, with corrupted byte runs rather than a clean parseable two-namespace document.
cmplog's I2SRandReplace splices CMP operands into the input at matching offsets to reconstruct exact token sequences.

operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so it cannot perform input-to-state substitution.
value_profile's CMP_MAP retains inputs whose operand pair is closer to matching than any previously seen, providing gradient feedback that cmplog lacks.

CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog unions cmplog and value_profile so its advantage over cmplog isolates the CMP_MAP gradient.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
union of cmplog and value_profile — edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the second xmlns declaration shares the prefix of the first (so the false-side equality check at L779-780 frees cur and returns NULL before linking) still showing vpc resolving the branch far above value_profile and cmplog would refute that reaching the non-NULL-nsDef append path is the decisive program feature.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The Pair 1 (value_profile delta) attribution to CMP_MAP gradient is inferred from feedback-mechanism reasoning, not from a lineage signal, since gradient retention leaves no dash-row trace in seed metadata.

signature.mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback retains near-miss inputs whose CMP operands are progressively closer to matching xmlns and prologue token comparisons, accumulating corpus progress toward a two-namespace document where node->nsDef is already populated at the second xmlns declaration.
signature.one_line: VP gradient accumulates corpus toward two-namespace document by retaining near-miss xmlns CMP intermediates

analysis.summary_one_line: An XML element carrying two or more namespace declarations so xmlNewNs sees a non-NULL node->nsDef and appends a second namespace

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same structural target (an element whose node->nsDef is already non-NULL when a second xmlns is declared), but here the winning edge is value-profile gradient feedback that preserves intermediate inputs whose CMP operands are progressively closer to matching the XML structural literals, letting the corpus climb toward a parseable two-namespace document.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 9/10 trials versus cmplog's 0/10; the CMP_MAP bucket feedback retains near-miss inputs (Hamming/prefix-distance closer to the xmlns and prologue token comparisons), so the corpus accumulates the structural progress needed to reach and append the second namespace at the blocker false side (avg hitcount winner=6, loser=0).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has the same I2SRandReplace mutator but only the edge-bucket feedback signal; it never retains the gradient intermediates, so across all 10 trials it stays blocked (resolved=0/10, blocked=10) and the branch hitcount is 0.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient: each integer/string CMP writes a distance-bucket into CMP_MAP, and an input producing an operand pair closer to matching than any seen before is preserved as new coverage; this gradient is the only technique differing between vpc and cmplog and is what lets the corpus accumulate toward the two-namespace document.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked side is the false branch of node->nsDef == NULL, taken only when the element already has a namespace, i.e. a second namespace is being added.
The false-side body that appends a second namespace (the winner-only region) is the else clause walking node->nsDef and linking cur via prev->next.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace substitutes logged element/attribute and xmlns literal bytes at matching input offsets, so the parser builds an empty element with a declared namespace, making nsNr != ctxt->nsNr true at the empty-element close.
signature.one_line: I2S plants xmlns-bearing element literals causing namespace count divergence

analysis.summary_one_line: Reach an empty element (<x/>) that carries namespace declarations so ctxt->nsNr changed, requiring both I2S and CMP_MAP gradient to assemble the tag

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A self-closing empty element whose start tag declares at least one namespace (e.g. a prefixed/xmlns attribute) so that ctxt->nsNr is incremented before the `/>` is consumed, making nsNr != ctxt->nsNr true at line 10185.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winner assembles a well-formed XML prolog plus angle-bracketed markup (`?>.<!`, `version="1.0"?>`, `<!DOCTY`) so the parser advances into xmlParseStartTag2/xmlParseElementStart and reaches the empty-element close where the namespace count diverges; vpc's I2SRandReplace splices logged comparands (element/attribute name and `xmlns`-style literal bytes) into the input so the upstream name/CMP gates pass.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser (no I2S) blocks 10/10 and never takes the true side; lacking I2SRandReplace it cannot substitute the exact logged literal bytes that the upstream name/attribute comparisons require, so it rarely builds an empty element that actually declares a namespace.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes one CMP operand for the other at matching input offsets, planting the exact literal bytes needed by upstream name/markup comparisons so the parser reaches the namespaced empty-element close; this technique is present in vpc but absent in the value_profile loser.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true side at line 10185 requires the namespace count to have changed during the element, inside the empty-element (`<x/>`) case.
The blocker sits under the self-closing empty-element guard `(RAW == '/') && (NXT(1) == '>')`.
Pair 2 isolates the I2S technique: vpc winner beats the value_profile loser.
Pair 1 isolates the value_profile technique: vpc winner beats the cmplog loser.
The value_profile loser never resolves the branch across all 10 trials.
The cmplog loser resolves the branch in zero of ten trials.
The winning vpc seed contains well-formed XML markup including a prolog and DOCTYPE opening.
I2SRandReplace substitutes one CMP operand for the other at matching input offsets, planting exact literal bytes.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile preserves an input whose CMP-operand pair is closer to matching than any seen before, climbing partial markup matches.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog lacks the CMP_MAP gradient feedback that value_profile provides.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Mutators**: naive's havoc + token stack. No `I2SRandReplace`.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness reaching this namespaced empty-element branch where cmplog (I2S only) resolves it as often as value_profile_cmplog, showing the value_profile CMP_MAP gradient was not separately required; or where value_profile alone matches vpc, showing I2S was not required.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Whether each single technique is independently necessary (true synergy) versus one technique dominating cannot be fully separated from the data, since vpc carries both and only one I2S-floor dash row appears in the verified winning chain.

signature.mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient preserves seeds whenever a CMP-operand pair is closer to matching markup literals (<?xml, DOCTYPE, attribute delimiters) than any seen before, letting vpc climb multi-character token comparisons toward a namespaced empty element that changes nsNr.
signature.one_line: VP gradient climbs multi-character markup literal comparisons toward namespace-count-changing empty element

analysis.summary_one_line: Reach an empty element (<x/>) that carries namespace declarations so ctxt->nsNr changed, requiring both I2S and CMP_MAP gradient to assemble the tag

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same namespaced empty element, but reached via incremental progress on the upstream byte-level comparisons (matching prefixes of markup literals such as <?xml, DOCTYPE, attribute names and quote/`/>` delimiters).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winner retains intermediate inputs that get progressively closer to the required markup literals; the CMP_MAP gradient preserves a seed whenever a CMP-operand pair is closer to matching than any seen before, letting vpc climb toward the multi-character markup tokens (`<!DOCTY`, `version=`) that gate entry to the empty-element branch.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser (no value_profile) blocks 9/10; it has I2S substitution but no Hamming/prefix-distance feedback, so partially-matching markup inputs earn no new coverage bucket and are discarded, and it stalls before assembling the full namespaced empty tag.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a coarse operand-distance bucket per CMP and rewards inputs that get closer to matching, preserving partial-match intermediates as corpus; this gradient is present in vpc but absent in the cmplog loser, so only vpc keeps climbing the multi-byte markup comparisons.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true side at line 10185 requires the namespace count to have changed during the element, inside the empty-element (`<x/>`) case.
The blocker sits under the self-closing empty-element guard `(RAW == '/') && (NXT(1) == '>')`.

Pair 1 isolates the value_profile technique: vpc winner beats the cmplog loser.
